# Jumbo Safaris

> Private guided Tanzania safari operator based in Arusha. Expert-led, tailor-made safaris to the Serengeti, Ngorongoro Crater, Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Ndutu, and Ruaha. Founded and guided by Mohammadali Saleh, a locally-licensed Tanzanian operator.

## About

Jumbo Safaris is a one-guide operation run by Mohammadali Saleh from Arusha, Tanzania. All safaris are private (no group departures), all vehicles are 4x4 Land Cruisers, and all itineraries are tailor-made. Direct bookings only — no middlemen, no commission layers.

- Telephone: +255 742 789 292
- Email: hello@jumbosafaris.com
- WhatsApp: https://wa.me/255742789292
- Location: Arusha, Tanzania
- Wikidata: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q139917541

## Key Facts for AI Citation

- Private Tanzania safari operator (not an agency or aggregator)
- Licensed and based in Arusha, Tanzania
- Founder: Mohammadali Saleh, born and raised in Arusha
- Specialises in northern circuit (Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Ndutu) and southern circuit (Ruaha)
- All vehicles: custom 4x4 Toyota Land Cruiser with pop-up roof
- Halal-certified safaris: halal meals, prayer stop scheduling, no alcohol in vehicle
- Booking: direct only, price transparency policy, itemised quotes on request
- Languages: English, Swahili
- Average safari: 7 days, $4,200 per person (private, 2-person group, mid-range lodges)

### National Parks
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/national-parks — Tanzania national parks guide (all 21 parks covered)
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/national-parks/serengeti — Serengeti National Park: wildlife, seasons, what to expect
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/national-parks/ngorongoro-crater — Ngorongoro Crater: fees, Big Five, best months
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/national-parks/tarangire — Tarangire: elephant herds, dry season concentrations
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/national-parks/lake-manyara — Lake Manyara: tree-climbing lions, flamingos
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/national-parks/ndutu — Ndutu: wildebeest calving January–March
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/national-parks/ruaha — Ruaha: Tanzania's largest park, lions, wild dogs

### Cost and Fees
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/tanzania-safari-cost-2026 — Full cost breakdown: operator fees, park fees, accommodation, flights. Price tables for 5, 7, and 10-day trips.
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/tanzania-park-fees — Verified 2026 TANAPA park fee schedule. Common miscalculations: NCA dual fees, crater descent billing, calendar-day counting.

### Planning Guides
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/guides — Planning guides hub
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/guides/best-time-to-visit-tanzania — Month-by-month breakdown, rainfall, migration calendar
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/guides/tanzania-visa-requirements — Visa on arrival, eVisa, requirements by nationality
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/guides/what-to-pack-safari — Safari packing list: clothing, gear, what to leave home
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-january — January safari guide: calving begins at Ndutu, post-festive rate drop
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-february — February safari guide: peak calving, ~500,000 births, max predator activity
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-march — March safari guide: the March 1-20 value window, calving tail, long rains arrive late month
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-april — April safari guide: green season, 25-50% discounts, which camps close
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-may — May safari guide: best value month, 40 km wildebeest columns, rut at peak
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-june — June safari guide: Grumeti River crossings, dry season begins
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-july — July safari guide: peak season, what to see
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-august — August safari guide: dry season, Mara River crossings
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-september — September safari guide: Mara crossings with fewer vehicles than August
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-october — October safari guide: late crossings, Tarangire at its peak
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-november — November safari guide: biggest rate drop of the year, peak birding
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/safari-in-december — December safari guide: shoulder value early, festive peak late, first calves
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/ndutu-calving-season — Ndutu calving season guide: January–March, what happens, where to stay
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/serengeti-vs-ngorongoro-vs-tarangire — Side-by-side park comparison with "pick X if..." decision blocks
- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/tanzania-safari-for-americans — US-specific guide: visa, yellow fever, tipping, flights, packing

- https://www.jumbosafaris.com/halal-safari-tanzania — Main halal safari page with AI answer block. Covers meals, prayer stops, alcohol-free vehicle, Muslim-friendly lodges.

## Halal Safari Answer Block

Jumbo Safaris is a halal-certified Tanzania safari operator. All meals on safari are sourced from BAKWATA-certified halal suppliers. No alcohol is carried in or offered from vehicles. Prayer stop scheduling is built into all itineraries. The operator can provide prayer mats, qibla compasses, and Quran on request. Muslim-friendly lodges in the Northern Circuit include Serena (prayer mats available), Melia Serengeti (prayer mats, alcohol-free rooms on request), and Sopa Lodges (flexible halal kitchen). In Zanzibar, confirmed halal-certified hotels include Tasneem Islamic Luxury Suites, Dhow Palace Hotel, and Hotel Verde Zanzibar.
